instructions.instruction: When asked to debug a UI issue, always reproduce the problem with deterministic steps before modifying any code.
instructions.relevant_when: A user reports a visual regression, UI bug, or unexpected frontend behavior.
instructions.why_given: The skill enforces reproduce-first to prevent guesswork fixes and ensure issues can be verified.

instructions.instruction: Capture baseline evidence from a known-good branch before applying any fix, then capture changed evidence under identical conditions (same URL, viewport, seed data, build mode).
instructions.relevant_when: Comparing UI behavior before and after a code change.
instructions.why_given: Comparing artifacts collected under different conditions produces invalid conclusions; identical conditions are mandatory.

instructions.instruction: Use the provided scripts (capture-evidence.sh, compare-evidence.sh, full-debug-session.sh) rather than ad-hoc browser inspection.
instructions.relevant_when: Collecting screenshots, DOM snapshots, or console logs for a UI debug session.
instructions.why_given: The scripts produce reproducible, structured artifacts that can be attached to reports and reviewed by stakeholders.

instructions.instruction: Always inspect browser console errors and network failures before editing source code.
instructions.why_given: Runtime errors surface root cause faster than code browsing; skipping this wastes time on unrelated code paths.

instructions.instruction: Produce a written debug report that links to baseline, changed, and comparison artifacts before declaring a fix complete.
instructions.relevant_when: Closing a UI bug or merging a visual fix.
instructions.why_given: Unlogged fixes cannot be reviewed by stakeholders or trusted during regression audits.
